rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory and hypothesis-development sections to support a core mechanism in the citing article: that gaze allocation tracks and influences utility and final choice. The citing paper specifically invokes Martinovici et al. (2023) for dynamic attention patterns toward preferred brands and for the idea that greater attention can amplify option value, which motivates H1 and the H2b alternative mechanism. This is more than a passing literature-list mention because it directly informs the conceptual setup the authors test against competing default-process models.
relation_description: The citing paper builds its attention-based account of default effects on prior evidence from Martinovici et al. that attention trajectories relate to utility accumulation and choice. It uses that prior finding as conceptual grounding for hypotheses about gaze bias and attention-linked value effects in its own default-choice context.
